title: Erik Per Sullivan Passed on 'Buckets of Money' for 'Malcolm in the Middle' Revival Role
body:
In a surprising move, the highly anticipated revival of the beloved sitcom "Malcolm in the Middle" has hit a snag, with young actor Erik Per Sullivan turning down a lucrative offer to reprise his role as Dewey, the mischievous and energetic middle child of the eccentric Malcolm family. According to Jane Kaczmarek, the actress who played the family's matriarch, Lois, Sullivan was presented with "buckets of money" to return to the show, but ultimately declined.

Sullivan's decision has left fans of the show disappointed, but not entirely surprised. As the youngest member of the Malcolm family, Dewey was a fan favorite, and his absence from the revival will undoubtedly be felt. However, Kaczmarek's comments suggest that Sullivan's decision was not taken lightly, and that he was offered a significant financial incentive to return to the show.

Kaczmarek's remarks also raise questions about the priorities of the show's creators and the cast members. While it's not uncommon for actors to turn down roles for personal or professional reasons, Sullivan's decision to pass on a major opportunity suggests that he may be committed to pursuing other interests and ambitions. As a Harvard University student, Sullivan is likely balancing his acting career with his academic pursuits, and it's possible that he felt that returning to the show would have interfered with his studies.

The "Malcolm in the Middle" revival, which is set to premiere in the coming months, has been generating significant buzz among fans of the original series. The show's creator, Linwood Boomer, has been working closely with the original cast to bring the beloved characters back to life, and the show's return has been highly anticipated. While Sullivan's absence will undoubtedly be a blow to fans, it's likely that the show will still be able to deliver a compelling and humorous take on the Malcolm family's adventures.

Kaczmarek's comments also highlight the challenges of revisiting a beloved series like "Malcolm in the Middle." When a show returns after a long hiatus, there are always risks involved, and it's not uncommon for cast members to move on to other projects. However, the show's creators have stated that they are committed to bringing back the original cast members, and it's likely that they will continue to work to persuade Sullivan to reprise his role as Dewey.

As the "Malcolm in the Middle" revival prepares to premiere, fans will be eagerly watching to see how the show adapts to Sullivan's absence. While it's clear that the show will still be able to deliver laughs and heart, the absence of Dewey will undoubtedly be a significant loss. Nevertheless, the show's creators have proven themselves to be talented and resourceful, and it's likely that they will find ways to make the show work despite Sullivan's departure. With its talented cast and witty writing, "Malcolm in the Middle" is sure to remain one of the most beloved and enduring sitcoms of all time, and fans will be eagerly awaiting its return.
